meaning: n.轭,轭状物,套,束缚,支配v.把...套上轭,结合,连接
examples: Patience and submission are the only comforts that are left to a poor people who groan under tyranny unless they are strong enough to break the yoke.
The introduction attempts to yoke the pieces together.
"Too much too soon either results in injury or burnout," says Mary Yoke, PhD, a faculty member in the kinesiology department at Indiana University in Bloomington.
Upon reaching this speed, you lift the yoke back, pitch up 10 degrees, and you're flying.
Other important animal tools include the saddle, bridle, the stirrup, the yoke, the whip.
